title: Log fetch attempts to fetch_history via shared helper
description:
Log every user-film-data fetch attempt to the existing Supabase fetch_history table for an audit trail of which egress pulled what, for whom, when.

Table (already created): fetch_history
- lbusername   — Letterboxd username fetched
- fetched_at   — timestamp of the fetch attempt
- fetch_status — 'success' | 'failure'
- method       — 'full_history' | 'rss' (grid backfill vs RSS refresh)
- origin       — 'local_worker' | 'production_endpoint' (residential vs Railway egress)

DECIDED: mechanism = shared DB-write helper, NOT an HTTP endpoint. Both local and Railway workers hold the Supabase service-role key, so both call the helper directly. Implement e.g. app/pipeline/fetch_log.py: log_fetch(supabase, lbusername, *, status, method, origin, fetched_at=None).

Requirements:
- A row is written on EVERY attempt, success AND failure — wrap the fetch so an exception still logs a failure row before propagating. A transient block (AccessDeniedError/429) is a failure row.
- Callable from BOTH paths with the right constants:
  * Domain A full-history grid backfill on the LOCAL worker -> origin=local_worker, method=full_history.
  * Domain B RSS refresh on the Railway worker (moviemaestro-a7k) -> origin=production_endpoint, method=rss.
- Log around the fetch call, not the DB upsert.
- Best-effort: a failed log-write must NOT abort an otherwise-successful data pull (warn + continue).

PLAN (resolve before coding):
1. fetched_at: caller-supplied (true attempt time) vs DB default now(). Prefer caller-supplied.
2. Enum enforcement: DB CHECK constraints on fetch_status/method/origin vs app-level. Recommend CHECK constraints.

Reference LETTERBOXD_DATA_FLOW.md for the egress/domain model.
acceptance_criteria: log_fetch() helper inserts one fetch_history row per attempt (success and failure) from both egress paths with correct method/origin; a logging failure never aborts a successful pull.

title: Implement RSS-based user film refresh (Domain B)
description:
Add an incremental user-history refresh that pulls from Letterboxd's per-user RSS feed (GET letterboxd.com/{user}/rss/) instead of the datacenter-blocked HTML grid. RSS survives Railway/datacenter egress; this is the ONLY moviemaestro change the current plan requires (see LETTERBOXD_DATA_FLOW.md, Domain B). Runs on the PRODUCTION worker.

Scope (as-built decisions):
- Fetch GET letterboxd.com/{lbusername}/rss/ via curl_cffi impersonate=chrome (already a dep). No pagination.
- Parse with stdlib xml.etree.ElementTree (namespaced tags). Filter <guid> to prefix 'letterboxd-watch-' (feed also carries letterboxd-list- items; verified 50 watch + 50 list in a live pull — do NOT assume a 50-item cap).
- Per watch item extract ONLY: slug (segment after 'film' in <link>; rewatch trailing /N/ falls away for free), letterboxd:filmTitle, letterboxd:memberRating (ABSENT tag when logged-but-unrated -> rating NULL), letterboxd:memberLike (Yes/No -> liked bool).
- INCLUDE unrated items (rating=NULL). watched_date/rewatch/tmdb:movieId are parsed-past, NOT persisted (out of scope, no new columns).
- Single batched upsert into UserFilms on_conflict (lbusername, film_slug) — same column set as user_films.py phase 1.
- Expose POST /refresh-user {lbusername} on Railway, bearer auth via WORKER_SHARED_SECRET. Synchronous (modeled on /backfill-film-years), returns {lbusername, watch_items, upserted}. Fetch failure (private/nonexistent/blocked, non-200) -> HTTP 502. Empty-but-valid feed -> 200, upserted=0.

Data integrity: both RSS (this endpoint) and grid backfill (Domain A, local) are upsert-only, no deletes, identical column set, same source of truth -> last-write-wins is safe regardless of run order. RSS 50-row window can never shrink backfilled history. Keep column sets identical; if they ever diverge, migrate UserFilms to per-column COALESCE-on-conflict like Films.upsert_film.

DECIDED (overrides earlier ticket text): do NOT refresh UserRatings histogram here — this endpoint only fetches latest UserFilms data.

Out of scope: Domain A grid backfill, Domain C film aggregates, new columns, fetch_history logging (moviemaestro-38k — leave the fetch as a clean seam).
acceptance_criteria: POST /refresh-user pulls a user's RSS feed from the Railway worker, filters to letterboxd-watch- guids, and upserts new/changed rows (including unrated -> rating NULL) into UserFilms via on_conflict (lbusername, film_slug). Does NOT touch UserRatings and does NOT hit any HTML route. Fetch failure returns 502; valid empty feed returns 200 upserted=0.

title: Unify UserRatings histogram refresh across scraper + RSS via shared refresh_user_ratings RPC
description: After any user-film update (full grid scrape OR RSS /refresh-user), UserRatings must reflect the user's full UserFilms distribution. Today only the grid scraper updates UserRatings (in-memory count from the full diary); the RSS path skips it. RSS can't count in-memory (only ~50 items) so it must aggregate the persisted UserFilms. To avoid two divergent implementations, unify both paths on a single Postgres RPC that recomputes the histogram from UserFilms. Matches the existing get_missing_films / upsert_film RPC pattern (all defined in bpdiscord supabase/migrations). Films/Domain-C metadata scraping stays local-only (HTML, Railway-blocked) and is out of scope.
design:
DECISION: full-replace histogram (delete user's UserRatings rows, re-aggregate from UserFilms). Fixes the stale-level bug the scraper currently punts on.

DEPENDS ON bpdiscord-xzi (the refresh_user_ratings RPC migration in bpdiscord/supabase/migrations) — must be deployed before this code ships or the RPC 404s.

WORK:
1. New app/pipeline/user_ratings.py: refresh_user_ratings(supabase, lbusername) -> supabase.rpc('refresh_user_ratings', {'p_username': lbusername}).execute()
2. user_rss.refresh_user_from_rss: after UserFilms upsert, call it (skip when upserted==0).
3. user_films.scrape_and_upsert_user_films: replace in-memory rating_counts + UserRatings upsert with a call to the same helper (behavior-preserving + stale-level fix; drops ~10 lines).
4. tests/fakes.py: add 'UserRatings' table + register refresh_user_ratings RPC (delete user's rows, re-aggregate UserFilms by rating) mirroring _upsert_film_rpc.
5. Tests: test_user_rss (histogram reflects FULL UserFilms incl. >50 pre-existing rows, not just the 50-item feed) + test_scrape_user (scraper still yields same histogram via helper).
Response shapes unchanged; bpdiscord proxy unaffected.
